methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
QuantumDiamonds is a seed-stage quantum computing company founded by Kevin Berghoff and Fleming Bruckmaier. The sector comprises 11 known comparable companies: 3 public entities (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private firms including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is inapplicable here. The quantum computing sector lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with similar valuation tiers—zero datapoints met the minimum threshold of three peer matches across narrow filters. A sector-wide median approach would introduce substantial noise given the heterogeneity of company stages, business models, and capital structures within the 11-firm universe.

The absence of robust comparable precedent means traditional triangulation cannot anchor a valuation range with confidence. QuantumDiamonds has secured €7M in seed funding plus a €152M facility commitment, establishing material capital access, but these represent fundraising outcomes rather than market-derived valuation signals. Without applicable peer multiples, discounted cash flow analysis, or sufficient precedent transactions, this analysis cannot support a specific valuation range. Any range would rest principally on investor negotiations, grant funding terms, and facility economics—factors outside traditional comparable-company methodology. Uncertainty is material and requires direct diligence into technology maturity, IP defensibility, and facility utilization assumptions.
